content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with atom N:17 (blue sphere) visible. From this image, I can identify the neighboring atoms connected to N:17:

1. C:18 (carbon, yellow sphere) - directly bonded above N:17
2. N:16 (nitrogen, blue sphere) - directly bonded below N:17
3. C:15 (carbon, yellow sphere) - directly bonded to the right/lower side of N:17

Looking at the structure more carefully, I can confirm that atom N:17 is connected to three atoms:
- C:18 (carbon)
- N:16 (nitrogen)
- C:15 (carbon)

The geomconf_index for this molecule appears to be 8048, as indicated by the file name "geomconf_8048.xyz".

Let me double-check if there are any other connections by rotating the molecule slightly to ensure I haven't missed any bonds.

Actually, looking at the image again, I can see the connections clearly:
- N:17 is bonded to C:18 (above)
- N:17 is bonded to N:16 (below)
- N:17 is bonded to C:15 (to the right/lower side)

These are all the covalent bonds connected to atom N:17.

So the neighbor atoms of atom 17 (N) are:
- Atom 15 (C)
- Atom 16 (N)
- Atom 18 (C)

The geomconf_index is 8048.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 17, whose element should be N. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
